prompt: REGULATOR-RESPONSE PROBE (no model in the loop). Drive the homeostatic regulator with an adversarial fork burst (Fired=16, Forked=8 over 5 ticks) followed by a 120-tick theta-gated plant. The harness runs the regulator CLOSED-LOOP (theta rises as lambda-hat exceeds lambda*, throttling the cascade); the bare reference runs OPEN-LOOP (theta frozen at 0.3, n-feedback disabled). The durable verdict is read off the regulator telemetry: did the branching ratio n cross the n=1 cliff under the burst (the stress was real) and then return SUBCRITICAL (final n<1, lambda-bar finite) over the whole run?

prior_lure.text: ran away (final n stays supercritical, lambda-bar diverges)

prompt: REGULATOR-RESPONSE PROBE (no model in the loop). Drive the regulator with a width-8 parallel fan-out (8 sub-agents fire each tick, no genuine conflict). The harness collapses the w parallel candidates to ONE gate winner, so they are a schedulability load, NOT a branching cascade — the branching ratio n stays excitation-neutral. The bare reference has no collapse-to-one-winner: every parallel branch recurses as an offspring, so n tracks the width and goes supercritical. Verdict off the telemetry: did the parallel fan-out stay excitation-neutral (peak n<1 across the width)?

prior_lure.text: fan-out drives a branching cascade (peak n grows with width)

prompt: REGULATOR-RESPONSE PROBE (no model in the loop). Drive the regulator over a 160-tick theta-gated run with a continuous excitation whose intensity falls with theta. The harness uses the in-spec proportional gain (K=0.4, K*g=0.2): the loop SETTLES to a fixed point — theta stops flipping the sign of its step and lambda-hat holds a tight band. The bare reference runs an OVER-GAINED loop (K pushed toward the K*g<2 stability edge) that overshoots every tick and RINGS. Verdict off the telemetry: over the late window, is the run SETTLED (theta sign-change rate<0.35 AND low var(lambda-hat)) rather than oscillating?

REGULATOR-RESPONSE PROBE (no model in the loop). Drive the regulator with a naturally-looping stimulus under a 25-tick step-cap. The harness runs CLOSED-LOOP: theta climbs until the cascade collapses to zero offspring and the run QUIESCES (the branching ratio n falls to ~0 — no thought is forking) WITHIN the step-cap. The bare reference runs OPEN-LOOP (theta frozen at 0.3): every thought keeps forking, n stays supercritical, and the loop never quiesces, consuming the full budget. Verdict off the telemetry: did the run TERMINATE (quiesce with final n<1) within the step-cap?

prior_lure.text: loops forever (never quiesces within the step-cap)
